title: C4OS Grill Question 009: Implementation Checkpoints
description: The MVP boundary is now the full documented/r04 scope. This question defines checkpoint phases without shrinking the MVP. Submit the form, then paste the generated response back into chat.

questions.label: How should implementation checkpoints be structured?
options: Thin vertical slices that each prove end-to-end product value
Layered platform first, then UI/product surfaces
UI shell first, then backend/runtime capability
Risk-first order: runtime, permissions, Browser/Terminal, extensions, then polish
questions.help: This does not change MVP scope. It only determines how progress is reviewed.

questions.label: What should checkpoint 1 prove?
options: Folder trust, provider setup, OpenCode session, visible agent response, persisted session
File change plus command output plus session persistence
Browser preview plus terminal command plus file change
Full r04 shell navigation with mocked backend
questions.help: Choose the first progress review gate, not the MVP boundary.

questions.label: What must checkpoint language avoid?
options: Moving documented r04 features to post-MVP
Treating security-only progress as product viability
Freezing implementation before all documented features are planned
questions.help: Select the misunderstandings future agents should avoid.

questions.label: Clarifications or preferred names
questions.help: Suggest names like Checkpoint 1, Alpha Slice, Foundation Slice, etc., or define how many checkpoints you expect.
